---
title: ""
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

<div style="text-align: center; padding: 40px 20px 20px;">

<div style="border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px; 
padding: 20px 40px; display: inline-block; margin-bottom: 25px;
background-color: white;">
<p style="margin: 4px 0; font-size: 1.1em;">
<strong>Faith Okaalo</strong>
</p>
<p style="margin: 4px 0; color: #555; font-size: 1.05em;">
University of Michigan
</p>
<p style="margin: 4px 0; color: #555; font-size: 1.05em;">
BIOSTAT 620 --- Introduction to Health Data Science
</p>
<p style="margin: 4px 0; color: #555; font-size: 1.05em;">
April 2026
</p>
</div>

<h1 style="font-size: 2.2em; color: #1a5276; 
text-decoration: underline; margin-bottom: 2px;">
Who Seeks Cancer Information?
</h1>

<h4 style="font-weight: normal; color: #555; 
margin-top: 0; margin-bottom: 25px;">
Demographic and Attitudinal Predictors Among U.S. Adults
</h4>

<img src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1576091160550-2173dba999ef?w=900&q=80"
     alt="Person searching for health information"
     style="width: 60%; border-radius: 10px; 
            box-shadow: 0 4px 15px rgba(0,0,0,0.15);
            margin-bottom: 25px;">

<p style="color: #2c3e50; line-height: 1.7; font-size: 1.05em; 
max-width: 700px; margin: 0 auto 20px;">
Using data from the Health Information National Trends Survey (HINTS 7),
a nationally representative survey of 7,278 U.S. adults conducted by the
National Cancer Institute in 2024, this project examines what predicts 
cancer information seeking and whether exposure to misleading health 
information plays a role.
</p>

<div style="margin-top: 20px; margin-bottom: 30px;">
<a href="report.qmd" style="background-color: #1a5276; color: white; 
padding: 10px 20px; border-radius: 6px; text-decoration: none; 
font-weight: 500; margin-right: 10px;">
View Report
</a>
<a href="data.qmd" style="background-color: white; color: #1a5276; 
padding: 10px 20px; border-radius: 6px; text-decoration: none; 
font-weight: 500; border: 2px solid #1a5276;">
About the Data
</a>
</div>

</div>

---

## About This Project

This website presents the final project for BIOSTAT 620. The analysis
uses data from the Health Information National Trends Survey Cycle 7
(HINTS 7), a nationally representative survey of 7,278 U.S. adults
conducted by the National Cancer Institute in 2024.

The primary research question guiding this analysis was to examine what
demographic and attitudinal factors predict cancer information seeking
among U.S. adults, and whether exposure to misleading or conflicting
health information is associated with seeking behavior.

## Key Findings

- Overall, 51.1% of U.S. adults sought cancer information in the past year
- A personal history of cancer was the strongest predictor (OR = 4.02, p < 0.001)
- Adults who frequently encountered conflicting health recommendations
were over five times more likely to seek information
- A striking misinformation paradox was observed --- those exposed to
more misleading health information were more likely to seek cancer
information, not less
- Education and income disparities in cancer information seeking
persisted even after adjustment

## Site Navigation

Use the navbar above to explore:

- [Report](report.qmd) --- Full analysis including introduction, methods, results, and conclusion
- [About the Data](data.qmd) --- Data source, variable descriptions, cleaning steps, and exploratory analysis

---

*Data source: National Cancer Institute. Health Information National
Trends Survey 7 (HINTS 7), 2024. Available at 
[hints.cancer.gov](https://hints.cancer.gov)*
